expectations.evidence: Multiple assert statements present: 'assert is_valid_email(email)' and 'assert not is_valid_email(email)'
The text 'inline_snapshot' does not appear in the response code, only in the prose explanation saying it would be 'overkill'
The word 'snapshot' appears in the prose: 'inline-snapshot would be overkill for scalar boolean returns'

expectations.text: Uses plain assert statements or pytest parametrize for this simple boolean function — does NOT use inline-snapshot, which would be overkill for True/False returns
expectations.evidence: Uses pytest.mark.parametrize with plain assert statements; explicitly avoids inline-snapshot and explains why it would be overkill

expectations.text: Tests both valid and invalid email inputs covering edge cases (no @, multiple @, empty parts)
expectations.evidence: test_valid_emails covers valid cases; test_invalid_emails covers 'no-at-sign' (no @), 'a@b@c' and '@@' (multiple @), '@domain', 'user@', '@', '' (empty parts)
